news.summary: Verve Therapeutics, a company specializing in genetic medicines for cardiovascular disease, has received Fast Track designation from the FDA for their drug VERVE-102. This drug is aimed at reducing low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C) in patient groups with hyperlipidemia and high cardiovascular risk. VERVE-102 is an investigational base editing medicine that targets the PCSK9 gene in the liver to lower LDL-C levels permanently. The drug is currently undergoing testing in the Phase 1b Heart-2 clinical trial, focusing on adult patients with heterozygous familial hypercholesterolemia (HeFH) and/or premature coronary artery disease (CAD) who require additional LDL-C reduction.
news.headline: Verve Therapeutics' VERVE-102, an in vivo base editing medicine targeting PCSK9, receives FDA Fast Track designation for cardiovascular disease, aiming to reduce LDL-C. Phase 1b trial targets patients with hyperlipidemia, with the goal of permanently turning off the PCSK9 gene, reducing LDL-C levels in the liver. #FDAFASTTRACK #VERVE102

news.summary: The article discusses a report by Allied Market Research on the LNG Carrier market, valued at $135.1 billion in 2023 and projected to reach $244.8 billion by 2033 with a 6.2% CAGR. The global LNG trade is expanding due to investments in LNG terminals and liquefaction plants. In 2023, the U.S. became the leading LNG exporter, surpassing Qatar and Australia with 91.2 million metric tonnes exported. Projects like Venture Global LNG's Plaquemines and the Golden Pass facility are expected to increase U.S. LNG production by 38 million tonnes annually. The maritime industry is focusing on sustainability through fleet modernization, with companies like MOL and ADNOC investing in LNG-fueled vessels. MOL aims to operate 90 LNG/methanol-powered vessels by 2030 for net-zero GHG emissions by 2050. ADNOC is investing up to $2.5 billion in new LNG carriers with advanced technologies for improved fuel efficiency and reduced carbon intensity, aligning with the company's 2045 Net Zero target.
news.headline: Global LNG Carrier Market valued at $135.1 billion in 2023, expected to reach $244.8 billion by 2033 at 6.2% CAGR. Major growth due to increased LNG trade and green shipping initiatives. U.S. emerged in 2023 as top LNG exporter. Fleet modernization and infrastructure development driving market expansion.

news.summary: In Saskatoon, Saskatchewan, on April 11, 2025, GFG Resources Inc. (TSXV: GFG) (OTCQB: GFGSF) has announced an update to its private placement offering. The company has decided to increase the offering to C$3.0 million and change the sale of premium flow-through units. Each Premium Unit will now include one common share and one whole share purchase warrant, allowing the holder to acquire an additional common share at C$0.28 within 24 months. Both the common shares and warrants in the Premium Units qualify as "flow-through shares" under the Income Tax Act (Canada).
news.headline: GFG increases private placement to C$3.0 million, amends Premium Units to include common shares and warrants.

news.summary: The surface exploration program in 2024 has yielded more impressive results, with high-grade gold rock samples being discovered. In the Wombat area, samples have shown gold concentrations reaching up to 360 grams per tonne (g/t Au), while in the RPM Regional area, samples have exhibited gold grades as high as 56.3 g/t Au. These findings demonstrate the continued success of the exploration efforts in identifying valuable gold deposits.
news.headline: Nova Minerals reports high-grade gold rock samples up to 360 g/t Au at Wombat & up to 56.3 g/t Au in RPM Regional area

news.summary: A hack on Morpho Labs' DeFi protocol led to the theft of $2.6 million in crypto assets, which were intercepted by a white hat MEV operator named c0ffeebabe.eth. Morpho Labs implemented a front-end update on April 10, leading to a vulnerability exploited by a hacker. The stolen funds were intercepted by the white hat hacker before being transferred to another wallet. Morpho Labs rolled back the update and ensured the safety of all funds in their protocol. The team identified and fixed the issue, confirming that users' assets are secure. The MEV operator has a history of recovering funds, including $5.4 million in a previous exploit. The team is yet to provide a detailed explanation of the incident.
news.headline: White hat hacker intercepts $2.6M in Morpho Labs breach; funds returned by MEV operator c0ffeebabe.eth in DeFi exploits.
